executive_summary: The Iran-US conflict has reached a new escalation threshold with IRGC claiming to strike the USS Abraham Lincoln carrier with ballistic missiles — the first known Iranian attack on a US aircraft carrier. Hezbollah has formally entered the war with attacks on Israeli missile defense sites near Haifa. Iranian strikes have expanded to Bahrain (offshore facility, ship fire killing 1), while Israel continues strikes on Tehran. UK has authorized US use of British bases for strikes on Iranian missile capabilities. The Strait of Hormuz remains effectively closed with multiple tanker attacks.
whats_new: IRGC claims ballistic missile strike on USS Abraham Lincoln carrier — first known Iranian attack on US aircraft carrier
Israeli-American airstrikes target Yazd and Isfahan in central Iran, expanding strikes beyond previous targets
Hezbollah formally enters war, announces targeting of Israeli missile defense site south of Haifa
Iranian missile hits Al-Makassed Arab Hospital in East Jerusalem — direct strike on medical infrastructure
Bahrain: Iranian missile debris causes deadly ship fire at Mina Salman port, 1 killed, 2 injured
Kuwait: Air raid sirens activated at US Ali Al Salem Air Base — first defensive alert in Kuwait
UK authorizes US to use British bases for limited strikes on Iranian missile storage and launchers
UNESCO condemns Israeli attack on girls' school in Minab, Iran — death toll now reported at 148-180
confirmed_events: Iran's Supreme Leader Khamenei confirmed dead — Iran declared 40-day mourning period
3 US troops killed, 5 wounded in Iranian retaliatory strikes on US bases in Iraq/Syria region — first direct US casualties
IRGC strikes 3 US/British oil tankers attempting to exit Persian Gulf through Strait of Hormuz
Hezbollah opens new front against Israel — rockets and drones target Haifa missile defense site
Bahrain: Iranian attack on offshore facility near Salman Port; missile debris causes ship fire, 1 dead
Israeli strikes on Tehran's Niloofar Square kill over 20 civilians including children at school
US has conducted over 1,000 strikes targeting Iranian assets since conflict began
Israeli attack on girls' school in Minab, Iran kills approximately 148-180 children
unverified_emerging: Russian strategist Dugin warns of World War 3 if Iran falls, signals potential Kremlin involvement (single source: Hindustan Times)
Israel military states 'not aware' of any Israeli/US strike on school in Minab — claims may be Iranian disinformation
Trump claims 'they tried to kill me twice, well — I caught Khamenei once' — unverified statement on assassination
Report: US and Israel attacked Iran approximately one week later than originally planned (single source: RIA Novosti)

people_travel: Cathay Pacific suspends Hong Kong-Dubai flights until Thursday, Hong Kong-Riyadh until Tuesday — 37 flights affected
Air raid sirens in Kuwait indicate heightened threat to US military personnel at Ali Al Salem Air Base
Red Color rocket alert issued for Achziv Beach in northern Israel — incoming threat from Lebanon
supply_chain: Strait of Hormuz effectively closed — IRGC strikes on tankers paralyzing shipping
Dubai and Abu Dhabi stock markets closed for two days trading suspension
Mina Salman port in Bahrain targeted — key Gulf maritime infrastructure under attack
Kuwaiti and Emirati stock markets closed amid escalating crisis
market_macro: Dubai Financial Market and Abu Dhabi Securities Exchange halt trading for two days — unprecedented market shutdown
Regional markets in freefall as Gulf states directly targeted
Economic disruption spreading beyond immediate conflict zones — Pakistan urges restraint, Oman hit for first time

outlook_24_72h.base_case: The conflict will likely continue escalating over the next 24-72 hours. With Khamenei's death confirmed, Iran is expected to pursue revenge operations through proxies (Hezbollah, Houthis, IRGC) while US/Israel intensify strikes on Iranian military infrastructure. The IRGC carrier strike, if confirmed, represents a major threshold that will likely trigger further US retaliation. Hezbollah's formal entry creates a third major front (Iran, Lebanon, Gulf). Risk of accidental escalation is HIGH.
escalation_risks: Confirmed IRGC strike on USS Abraham Lincoln carrier triggers major US retaliatory response
Russian involvement signals — Dugin warning suggests Kremlin may intervene if Iran weakens further
Iranian strikes on Gulf states (Bahrain, Kuwait, UAE) could draw direct US/Gulf state retaliation
Hezbollah openingLebanon front expands conflict geographically
Hospital and school strikes create humanitarian crisis increasing international pressure
US ammunition shortage concerns may force acceleration or negotiation
outlook_24_72h.de_escalation_pathways: De-escalation appears unlikely in the immediate 24-72h window. Iran has stated it will exercise 'no limit' to self-defense. Trump has given Iranian forces an ultimatum to surrender weapons or face destruction. The only plausible pathways would be: (1) major Iranian military collapse prompting surrender offer, (2) international mediation from Russia/China intervening to halt escalation, or (3) accidental pause from mutual exhaustion — none appear probable in the near term. The conflict trajectory points toward continued intensification.
